tasks.title: Secure sensitive credentials using Kubernetes Secrets
tasks.description: Modify the infrastructure setup to store all sensitive credentials (PostgreSQL, Redis, S3/R2) in Kubernetes Secrets instead of ConfigMaps. For future production deployments, plan for a robust secret management solution.
subtasks: Update Task 1's details to provision Kubernetes Secrets for 'sigma1-infra-endpoints' instead of a ConfigMap.
Ensure all affected services (Equipment Catalog, RMS, Finance) are configured to consume credentials from the new Kubernetes Secrets.
Research and propose a long-term secret management solution (e.g., External Secrets Operator, HashiCorp Vault) for production environments.
Verify that no sensitive credentials remain in plain-text ConfigMaps.
acceptance_criteria: Kubernetes Secrets are used for PostgreSQL, Redis, and S3/R2 credentials.
All services successfully connect to their respective infrastructure using credentials from Secrets.
No sensitive credentials are found in any ConfigMaps.
A proposal for a production-grade secret management solution is documented.

tasks.title: Implement comprehensive API Authentication and Role-Based Access Control
tasks.description: Integrate an Identity Provider (IdP) and implement robust authentication middleware and Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) for all administrative and state-mutating API endpoints across the Equipment Catalog, RMS, and Finance services.
subtasks: Research and select an appropriate Identity Provider (e.g., Keycloak, Auth0, AWS Cognito) for integration.
Implement authentication middleware (e.g., JWT validation) in Rust (Axum) and Go (gRPC/REST Gateway) services.
Define roles and permissions for administrative, financial, and AI agent users.
Enforce RBAC policies on all POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE endpoints and sensitive GET endpoints (e.g., POST /api/v1/catalog/products, POST /api/v1/invoices, POST /api/v1/equipment-api/checkout).
Update API documentation to reflect authentication and authorization requirements.
Add unit and integration tests for authentication and RBAC enforcement.
acceptance_criteria: All administrative and state-mutating API endpoints require valid authentication tokens.
Unauthorized access attempts are correctly rejected with 401/403 errors.
RBAC policies are enforced, ensuring users only access resources and perform actions they are authorized for.
Authentication and authorization mechanisms are documented.

tasks.title: Implement strict file upload validation and sanitization for product images
tasks.description: Enhance the S3/R2 image upload functionality in the Equipment Catalog service (Task 3) to include strict validation for file types, size limits, and secure naming conventions to prevent malicious uploads and denial of service.
subtasks: Implement server-side MIME-type validation, allowing only specific image formats (e.g., JPEG, PNG, WebP).
Enforce file size limits (e.g., max 5MB per image).
Implement randomized renaming of uploaded files to prevent path traversal and overwrite attacks.
Integrate these validations into the image upload process for the Equipment Catalog service.
Add unit and integration tests to verify upload restrictions.
acceptance_criteria: Only allowed image MIME types can be uploaded.
Files exceeding the size limit are rejected.
Uploaded files are renamed to a secure, non-guessable format.
Malicious file uploads (e.g., executables, oversized files) are blocked.

tasks.title: Implement structured security audit logging for critical operations
tasks.description: Integrate structured security audit logging into the Rust and Go services (RMS and Finance) to record sensitive business operations, including actor identity, action performed, timestamp, and resource ID.
subtasks: Define a standardized audit log format (e.g., JSON) for all critical operations.
Implement audit logging for all state-mutating operations in the RMS service (Opportunities, Projects, Inventory, Crew, Delivery).
Implement audit logging for all state-mutating operations in the Finance service (Invoice creation, status updates, payments).
Ensure logs include actor identity (once authentication is in place), action, timestamp, and relevant resource identifiers.
Configure log output to a centralized logging system (e.g., stdout for Kubernetes to pick up, then forward to Loki/Elasticsearch).
Add unit tests to verify audit log generation for critical paths.
acceptance_criteria: All critical state-mutating operations generate a structured audit log entry.
Audit logs contain actor identity, action, timestamp, and resource ID.
Audit logs are correctly forwarded to the configured logging sink.
No sensitive information is inadvertently exposed in audit logs.
